svg.text: 06 - Data Model and Storage Schema
What this covers: relational core, JSON flexibility, geospatial model, and audit-critical records.
Data Philosophy
Relational backbone for integrity + JSONB for adaptive travel data + PostGIS for location-aware planning and routing.
Identity + Trip Core
users
id/email/name/timestamps + preferences/profile JSONB (travel DNA, notification windows).
trips
user FK + destination scope + status lifecycle (planning/confirmed/active/completed/archived).
transport_plan, flight_details, planning_state JSONB support evolving planning state.
itineraries
versioned per trip, active flag, summary JSONB for quick renders.
itinerary_days
day_number/date/title/location_point/day_type transport/accommodation/condition snapshots.
tightness scoring + lock semantics preserve user intent under adaptation pressure.
activity_instances
day FK + optional attraction FK + timing, sensitivity, provider metadata, booking state.
status lifecycle supports planned/active/completed/skipped/swapped tracking.
Practical modeling decisions
- UUID keys for distributed-safe references
- explicit enum-like text fields for state readability
- unique constraints on day_number per itinerary and version per trip
Knowledge + Content Tables
attractions
destination/region/type arrays + condition profiles (ideal/acceptable/dealbreaker JSONB).
location_point indexed with GIST for proximity and route-near search.
providers
activity_types GIN index + quality/pricing/contact URLs for marketplace logic.
hidden_gems
detour-level discovery objects with type taxonomy and insider tips.
stories
cultural/contextual narratives with optional audio and consumption-context metadata.
conversation memory
conversations + messages tables persist prompt context and structured interaction artifacts.
token_count supports cost attribution and optimization loops.
Condition + adaptation persistence
condition_records store raw/normalized snapshots by source and forecast horizon.
swap_suggestions preserve recommendation rationale, impact scores, and user response.
daily_briefings keep content + conditions snapshot + delivery/view timestamps for auditability.
Geospatial Query Patterns
Core spatial operations enabled by PostGIS:
- attractions within radius (ST_DWithin)
- point-to-point distances for route scoring
- route corridor search against LineString buffers
Schema quality controls
- GIST indices for location columns
- GIN indices for arrays (types/activity categories)
- btree indices on high-cardinality FK/status/date columns
Migration discipline
Alembic-only schema evolution. No direct production DDL edits.
Forward + rollback plans required per release candidate.
Data retention rationale
Historical condition and swap/briefing records support debugging and model tuning.
Conversation logs support explainability and user-facing "why" transparency.
Entity Relationship Narrative (Practical Read)
User owns trips; trip owns itinerary versions; itinerary owns days; day owns activity instances.
Activity instances may reference attractions but keep denormalized execution fields for day-specific behavior.
Swap suggestions link two itinerary days plus rationale metrics; acceptance mutates itinerary state and downstream briefings.
Condition records are independent time-series snapshots consumed by condition service and AI generation tasks.
Briefings snapshot both generated content and condition context to preserve historical truth of what user saw.
Data Integrity Risks to Guard Against
- orphaned records when itinerary/day/activity deletions are not cascade-safe
- race conditions between swap acceptance and concurrent itinerary edits
- inconsistent timezone handling for day boundaries and briefing schedules
- schema drift between JSONB payload writers across services
Testing Priorities
migration tests, FK cascade tests, geospatial query correctness, swap transaction atomicity, historical snapshot immutability.
Next recommended read: 07 - External Integrations and Fallbacks.
